Notice Of Motion; Motion To Compel Evidence; Declaration Of Plaintiff, Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Jan-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DANIEL POWELL Notice Of Motion; Motion To Compel Evidence; Declaration Of Plaintiff, Proof Of Service

The motion is denied. First, plaintiff failed to meet and confer regarding the discovery request, which is mandatory. (Townsend v. Super. Ct. (1998) 61 Cal.App.4th 1431, 1435 ["The Discovery Act requires that, prior to the initiation of a motion to compel, the moving party declare that he or she has made a serious attempt to obtain 'an informal resolution of each issue.'"].) Second, the motion does not "set forth specific facts showing good cause for the discovery sought by the demand." (Code Civ. Proc., 2031.310, subd. (b)(1).) The court agrees that plaintiff's First Amended Complaint does not set forth a viable claim and therefore, the court cannot discern whether the discovery request is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (Calcor Space Facility, Inc. v. Superior Ct. (1997) 53 Cal.App.4th 216, 223-224.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301/JMQ)
